title: Contract Fulfilled in Letter, Broken in Spirit

scenes.title: Preparation
scenes.goal: Prepare for contract loophole — ritual space, magical parameters, emotional reality
scenes.conflict: Lirien must prepare to stop the heart of the woman she loves

valueShift.to: We are about to enact it, and the plan requires me to kill the person I love

fiveCommandments.incitingIncident: Ritual preparation begins — ward-stones placed, magical parameters established
fiveCommandments.complication: The resonance bond is essential for monitoring but means Lirien will feel Kaelith die
fiveCommandments.crisis: Proceed knowing she will feel the death through the bond, or find another way
fiveCommandments.climax: Lirien confirms readiness, Kaelith gives verbal consent, preparations complete
fiveCommandments.resolution: Ready. The loophole depends on precision, trust, and the bond

beats: ACTION: Ritual space preparation — ward-stones, timing calculations, operative precision
DIALOGUE: Final exchange — clinical language containing subtext of love and trust
INTERNAL: Worst-case scenarios — loophole becomes murder if resuscitation fails
REACTION: Stillness before beginning — eyes meeting, bond carrying determination and terror

scenes.title: Execution
scenes.goal: Execute the loophole — stop heart, satisfy binding, restart heart
scenes.conflict: Millisecond precision from a woman who will feel her love die through the bond

valueShift.to: Trust proven through the most literal surrender

fiveCommandments.incitingIncident: Lirien initiates — fae magic through the bond targeting heartbeat
fiveCommandments.complication: Binding resists — enforcement mechanism fights the loophole
fiveCommandments.crisis: Push through resistance at risk of damaging the bond, or abort
fiveCommandments.climax: Heart stops. Bond goes silent. Binding registers fulfillment and dissolves
fiveCommandments.resolution: Lirien channels everything and restarts Kaelith's heart

beats: ACTION: Initiation — fae magic through bond, precision targeting cardiac conduction
ACTION: Heart stops, bond goes SILENT, binding dissolves in cascade
ACTION: Resuscitation — everything channeled through bond, heart restarts
REACTION: Kaelith gasps back, bond floods with mutual sensation

scenes.title: Aftermath
scenes.goal: Process aftermath — binding gone, bond remains, love proven free
scenes.conflict: Emotional aftermath of literal death and resurrection

valueShift.to: What remains is only real — and we choose to keep it

fiveCommandments.incitingIncident: Quiet after resurrection — both on floor, breathing, bond carrying relief
fiveCommandments.complication: Lirien checks — binding truly gone, body different, feelings unchanged
fiveCommandments.crisis: Accept the proof that the love is real, or continue doubting
fiveCommandments.climax: Kaelith speaks: 'The binding is gone. You're still here.'
fiveCommandments.resolution: Together because they choose to be. The cage is open. Both stay

beats: INTERNAL: Body-check — cataloguing binding absence, what remains is pure and chosen
DIALOGUE: 'The binding is gone. You're still here.' / 'I was always going to be here.'
ACTION: Physical reconnection — hands, foreheads, contact different without binding
REACTION: Return to mission reality (Quietus, Thalen) but hand doesn't leave. Close: 'What remains is only real.'

romanceBeat.beat: Trust Proves Itself — Kaelith Consents to Death and Resurrection
romanceBeat.description: The ultimate vulnerability: allowing the person you love to stop your heart, trusting they will restart it. When the binding dissolves and love remains, proof is complete.

fantasyBeat.beat: Contract Fulfilled Through Temporary Heartbeat Cessation — Loophole Exploited
fantasyBeat.description: The kill contract's exact wording — 'cessation of heartbeat' as state not duration — is exploited through temporary cardiac arrest. Binding architecture resists but dissolves when the letter of the contract is satisfied.

emotionalArc.start: Terrified precision — preparing to stop the heart of the woman she loves
emotionalArc.pivot: The moment Kaelith's heart restarts and the bond floods back
emotionalArc.end: Stunned, fierce relief — binding gone, love remains, proof complete

T5-ToneContract.notes: Maintained darkly romantic, emotionally restrained but intense tone throughout. Magic scenes treated as intimate and destabilizing per tone contract. No sentimentality — emotion earned through action and sensation.

T6-StakesEscalation.notes: Stakes at maximum — literal life and death. The loophole's margin of error (4-7 seconds) creates constant escalation pressure.

T7-BlackMoment.notes: Recovery from Ch16 black moment completed. Trust rebuilt through ultimate surrender — the inverse of betrayal.

T8-InternalExternalResistance.notes: External: binding enforcement mechanism resists the loophole. Internal: Lirien's fear of failure, analytical mind running worst-case scenarios.

T10-SandersonsLaws.notes: All magic uses established mechanics only: resonance bond (Ch11), fae precision magic (established), contract wording loophole (Ch14/FACT-008), binding dissolution cascade. No new abilities introduced.

T12-DualArcInterleave.notes: Fantasy action (contract dissolution) IS romance proof. The dual arcs merge completely — stopping the heart fulfills the contract AND proves the love.

T13-TensionMapping.notes: Tension level 9 maintained throughout. Preparation builds dread, execution delivers maximum tension, aftermath provides earned recovery.

T15-Foreshadowing.notes: FS-008 (Oathbrand removal proof of precision), FS-011 (Seep tacit support), FS-012 (loophole research payoff) all serviced. Seeds planted for Ch21 Quietus approach.

T16-SymbolicMotifs.notes: MOT-001 (pallor/silver on Kaelith's arm), MOT-002 (bond as living current), MOT-003 (water/current throughout Lirien's narration), MOT-004 (cage opening, door/threshold). Architecture kept exclusively to Kaelith's speech.

T17-VoiceFingerprinting.notes: Lirien's voice maintained throughout: water metaphors, classification language, field-note fragments, operative precision. No military/percussive rhythm (Kaelith's domain). Sentences elongate under emotional pressure.

T18-ShowDontTell.notes: All emotion conveyed through sensation, bond physics, physical action, and internal processing. No emotion labels. The bond silence conveys loss better than any stated grief.

T20-EmotionalThroughline.notes: Lirien's emotional state: terrified precision → desperate determination → overwhelming relief → quiet proof. No repeated dominant emotion from Ch19.

T21-MRUStructure.notes: MRU pattern maintained in key moments: external stimulus (heart stops) → emotional reaction (bond silence) → physical reflex (vision darkens, hands shake) → rational action (pour everything into bond) → speech ('I was always going to be here').

T23-PacingRhythm.notes: Three-beat structure: slow preparation (dread), fast execution (crisis), slow aftermath (processing). High tension balanced by recovery in final scene.

T24-SubtextInDialogue.notes: Clinical dialogue in SCN-56 carries emotional subtext throughout. Kaelith's consent is love disguised as operational confirmation. Final dialogue in SCN-58 has surface and depth aligned for the first time.

T25-ThematicArgument.notes: Act III forces hardest version of trust question: can trust survive literal death at a lover's hands? Answer earned through action, not declaration.

serviced.description: Oathbrand removal referenced as proof of what Lirien's hands can do — precision and healing, not just killing
Seep's tacit support for loophole acknowledged — contract language always contained exploitable phrasing
Contract loophole fully executed — cessation of heartbeat as state, not duration, binding dissolved

planted.description: Quietus approach seeded — three days travel, god-seal reinforcement still needed, Thalen still operating
Lirien's unbound magic — precision and power without contract interference, available for Quietus confrontation

characterPositions.CHAR-001: Forward shelter, Ashborne relay network, between Rivenmarch and Quietus
characterPositions.CHAR-002: Forward shelter, same location as CHAR-001

continuityNotes.bondState: Open, full, cleaner post-dissolution — binding interference removed, resonance sharper
continuityNotes.contractState: DISSOLVED. Binding fulfilled through temporary heartbeat cessation. Contract architecture collapsed. Lirien is unbound.
continuityNotes.pallorProgression: Silver past elbow, three fingers frozen left hand, cold tracking bone along ulna. Unchanged by contract dissolution.
continuityNotes.timelinePosition: Day ~31-32 (three days after Ch19 Rivenmarch, en route to Quietus)

sceneValueShifts.SCN-56: confident planning → crushing emotional reality of what the plan requires
sceneValueShifts.SCN-57: trust tested → trust proven through death and resurrection
sceneValueShifts.SCN-58: contract dissolved → love proven real and freely chosen

chapterStart.CHAR-002: Terrified precision, operative mode at maximum, preparing to stop beloved's heart
chapterStart.CHAR-001: Determined trust, steady resolve, consenting to vulnerability

chapterEnd.CHAR-002: Stunned relief, tears unclassified, love proven real. First experience of unbound choice
chapterEnd.CHAR-001: Fierce tenderness, structural doubt collapsed, immediately redirecting to mission (Quietus)
